_metadata.purpose: Fixed Ed25519 test private key for golden-receipt cross-track byte-identity demonstration
_metadata.guard_recommendation: Follow-on hardening (non-gating): repository-level deny-rule (e.g. deny.toml / pre-commit grep) that rejects any non-fixture reference to the seed string or to the derived private-key bytes.

derivation._note: Deterministic derivation. All three tracks MUST produce byte-identical private-key seed bytes and Ed25519 public-key bytes given the seed string below.

cross_track_equivalents.rust: use ed25519_dalek::SigningKey; let seed = sha2::Sha256::digest(b"witseal-v0.2-golden-receipt-test-key-0001"); let sk = SigningKey::from_bytes(&seed.into()); let pk = sk.verifying_key();
cross_track_equivalents.python: import hashlib; from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ed25519 import Ed25519PrivateKey; seed = hashlib.sha256(b'witseal-v0.2-golden-receipt-test-key-0001').digest(); sk = Ed25519PrivateKey.from_private_bytes(seed); pk = sk.public_key()
cross_track_equivalents.typescript_noble: import {ed25519} from '@noble/curves/ed25519'; import {sha256} from '@noble/hashes/sha256'; const seed = sha256(new TextEncoder().encode('witseal-v0.2-golden-receipt-test-key-0001')); const pk = ed25519.getPublicKey(seed);

captured_resolved_values._note: These three fields are persisted when the reproduction binary first runs and stores the resolved values. Until then, treat the `<derive: ...>` placeholders in `derivation` as the source of truth — every track re-derives independently and must agree.

_security_posture.rationale: Test fixture only. Private-key material in repo is intentional for cross-track byte-identity reproducibility. NEVER reuse this seed or derived key for any real signing operation. The seed string and derived bytes are forbidden in non-fixture code paths.
_security_posture.reuse_prohibition_scope: All WitSeal track implementations. Test fixtures derived from THIS seed MUST live only under `corpus/v0.2/golden_receipt/` or equivalent test-fixture trees in sister repos.
